title: German Beetroot Sauerbraten
description: A unique twist on the traditional German dish, featuring fermented flavors and beetroot. Serve with a side of braised red cabbage for a hearty meal.
instructions: 2. Peel and chop the beetroot into 1-inch pieces.
3. In a large bowl, combine the beetroot, sauerkraut, caraway seeds, flour, sugar, vinegar, water, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
4. Transfer the mixture to a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot.
5. Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
6. Braise the mixture for 2 hours, or until the beetroot is tender.
7. Serve the sauerbraten hot, garnished with fresh parsley, with a side of braised red cabbage.
8. Slice the bun into slices and serve on the side.
